name: Beaten Biscuits
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix the flour, salt, sugar, and baking powder in a bowl or food processor fitted with a metal blade.
2. Add the shortening and cut in until the mixture is the consistency of coarse meal.
3. Pour in the milk and stir or process just until the dough holds together. If it is dry or crumbly, add more milk. If it is too wet, add more flour.
4. Knead briefly in the food processor, then turn out onto a floured board.
5. Roll out the dough until it is 1/2 inch thick.
6. Cut with a 1-1/4-inch biscuit cutter into small rounds.
7. Prick each round with a fork, making two parallel sets of holes in the biscuits.
8. Fold the dough in half and roll out again until all scraps are gone and about 100 biscuits have been made.
9. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
10. Place the biscuits on a lightly greased baking sheet.
11. Bake for 30 minutes, or until crisp but not browned. They should open easily when split with a fork.
12. Keep tightly covered in a tin or in the freezer; split before serving.
